overrideReason: Jul 16: confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings fell to 8, the lowest level in three weeks and down from 15 the previous day, with 7 of the 8 transits using the riskier Iranian route rather than the US-protected Omani corridor, per Kpler ship-tracking data; the US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in force after the ceasefire broke down Jul 14, and JMIC advisories continue to warn the strait is too dangerous for normal commercial transit. Verified transit data lags several days behind.

reportedTransits.source: Kpler ship-tracking data (via Kpler's official reporting, picked up by Marine Log, Times of Israel, MarineLink): confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings fell to 8 on Jul 16, the lowest level in three weeks, down from 15 the previous day; 7 of the 8 transits used the Iranian route; no shadow fleet crossings recorded
